commands.review_summary:
The PR filters plugin session-state doctor scans to entries with route-state fields, memoizes resolved routes by agent id, disables manifest model-id normalization in the scan path, and adds a focused regression/performance test.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main resolves routes for every session-store key before filtering, and the PR body reports a real large-store doctor run where that path failed to complete promptly; I did not execute the CLI repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is needed because the PR already contains the focused source and test changes; the remaining action is normal maintainer review and exact-head CI.

target.title: fix(doctor): skip empty entries and memoize routes in plugin session repairs
target.body:
## Symptom

`openclaw doctor` hangs indefinitely (5+ minutes, 100% CPU, ~1.2 GB RSS) on installations with sizable session stores. Visible output stops after the 'Plugin registry refreshed' note. With the default 30-day `session.maintenance.pruneAfter` and a relaxed `maxEntries`, a heavy CLI user can easily accumulate several hundred entries, which is enough to trigger this.

## Root cause

`runPluginSessionStateDoctorRepairs` (in `src/commands/doctor-session-state-providers.ts`) has two compounding inefficiencies that turn an O(N) loop over the session store into something far worse:

1. **It resolves routes for every entry.** `Object.keys(params.store).map(... resolveConfiguredDoctorSessionStateRoute ...)` runs once per session key. `scanEntryForOwner` can only produce a repair or manual-review finding when the row exposes at least one of the fields covered by `entryMayContainPluginSessionRouteState` (`providerOverride`, `modelOverride`, `agentHarnessId`, `agentRuntimeOverride`, `cliSessionBindings`, `cliSessionIds`, `authProfileOverride*`, `modelOverrideSource`, `liveModelSwitchPending`, `model`, `modelProvider`). Resolving a route for any entry that fails that predicate is pure waste, and the store typically has many bare entries (every short CLI session leaves a stub row).
2. **The route is a function of agentId, not sessionKey.** `resolveConfiguredDoctorSessionStateRoute` uses `sessionKey` only to derive the agent id via `resolveSessionAgentId`; every `agent:main:*` key resolves to the same route, so building the map per-sessionKey recomputed the same work hundreds of times.
3. **`parseModelRef` inside `scanEntryForOwner` invokes plugin-manifest model-id normalization.** With only `allowPluginNormalization: false` set, `normalizeStaticProviderModelId` still calls `normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest`, which calls `loadPluginMetadataSnapshot` from scratch on every call (memoization only kicks in when a 'current' snapshot is bound to process state, which it is not during doctor). Each `parseModelRef` then costs roughly the file I/O of loading the plugin metadata snapshot, ~40 ms each on this machine. With ~670 calls in the scan, that is ~27 s of pure manifest reloading per doctor run. `scanEntryForOwner` never reads the manifest-normalized model id, only the provider id, so the manifest path is unused work.

## Fix

Two commits, one helper file:

1. Filter `params.store` with `entryMayContainPluginSessionRouteState` before resolving routes, and memoize `resolveConfiguredDoctorSessionStateRoute` by agent id within the surviving entries. Both helpers already exist in `doctor-session-state-providers.ts`; this rearranges the existing call shape without changing what `scanSessionRouteStateOwners` sees.
2. Pass `allowManifestNormalization: false` alongside the existing `allowPluginNormalization: false` on all three `parseModelRef` call sites in this file. `normalizeStaticProviderModelId` already short-circuits to `normalizeBuiltInProviderModelId` in that case.

`scanSessionRouteStateOwners` still receives every entry it could possibly find anything for; no finding the pre-fix code would have produced is lost.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw doctor --yes --non-interactive` hangs indefinitely on a real session store with ~800 entries (no visible progress after 5+ minutes; gateway stays unmodified, no further notes ever print).
- Real environment tested: `openclaw` 2026.5.22, macOS 26.5 (Apple silicon), Node 25.9. Real OpenClaw state directory at `~/.openclaw` with an 18 MB `sessions.json` (~800 entries), gateway-mode local, ~120 plugins enabled (12 active), default `session.maintenance.pruneAfter` of 30 days.
- Exact steps run after this patch:
  1. `pnpm build` against this branch (clean tsdown + runtime-postbuild).
  2. Cleaned the real store down to 230 entries via `openclaw sessions cleanup --enforce --fix-missing` (configured `maxEntries: 500`, `pruneAfter: 14d`, `mode: enforce`) so the comparison was not affected by missing-transcript pruning.
  3. `time openclaw doctor --yes --non-interactive` from `/tmp`.
- Evidence after fix (live terminal output, last few lines only):

```
‚îÇ
‚îî  Doctor complete.

real    0m56.004s
user    0m26.166s
sys     0m15.707s
```

- Per-contribution breakdown (instrumented locally on the same store; trace removed before commit):

```
contribution                            before fix   after fix
doctor:state-integrity                    38621 ms     2438 ms   (-94 %)
doctor:gateway-daemon                     15376 ms    15350 ms   (unchanged, separate concern)
doctor:structured-health-repairs          12738 ms    13206 ms   (unchanged, separate concern)
doctor:claude-cli                         10363 ms    10098 ms   (unchanged, separate concern)
doctor:session-transcripts                 3991 ms     3917 ms
doctor:gateway-health                      3269 ms     3261 ms
total doctor wall-clock                   89539 ms    53410 ms
```

Inside `runPluginSessionStateDoctorRepairs` on the same store (230 entries, 224 filtered in, 3 owners): scan loop fell from ~36 s to <300 ms; route resolution fell from 1 call costing 2.2 s and being reissued per session-key (the old code) to 1 cached call.

## Tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-session-state-providers.test.ts src/commands/doctor-state-integrity.test.ts` ‚Äî 42 / 42 pass (11 existing provider tests, 30 state-integrity tests, 1 new test).
- New test (`skips entries without plugin route state and memoizes routes per agentId`) builds a 200-entry store where two entries carry `agentHarnessId: codex-cli` and asserts the codex owner fires its 'pinned runtime' repair on those two entries, the user-declined confirmation produces no changes, and the run completes in well under 2 s (pre-fix would have exceeded 5 minutes on a real cfg; this test uses a synthetic cfg so the wall-clock guard is conservative).
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/commands/doctor-session-state-providers.ts src/commands/doctor-session-state-providers.test.ts` ‚Äî 0 warnings, 0 errors.
- `pnpm format:check` on touched files ‚Äî clean.
- `pnpm tsgo:core --noEmit` ‚Äî clean.
- `pnpm build` ‚Äî clean, produces the same dist surface.

Real `gateway`/`channel`/E2E proof skipped per AGENTS.md scoping; this change is narrow to one core doctor helper with no protocol, plugin SDK, runtime, or channel surface touched.
target.author: joeyfrasier
